Patch Set 1:

Thanks Andreas for the detailed review comments. I am going through them.

Patch Set 1:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 1:

I had a question about printing Lustre related information for files. So if the user inputs --printf "%Lc:%LS:Lo:Li" as the format string, then for files with normal striping layout the information can be printed like this: 3:1048576:0,1,2:1. here Lc denotes stripe count, LS stripe size, Lo ost indices and Li starting ost index. So my question is what is a good way to represent/print out this information for composite layout files since values for each component will be different ?

Patch Set 1:

"lfs getstripe" prints the stripe count/size/index of the last initialized component for these parameters on PFL files.  The presence of the earlier components (with fewer stripes, smaller stripe size, etc) is not very useful when migrating files and/or mirroring the layout.
Removed reviewer Rick Mohr.

Patch Set 1:

> "lfs getstripe" prints the stripe count/size/index of the last
 > initialized component for these parameters on PFL files.  The
 > presence of the earlier components (with fewer stripes, smaller
 > stripe size, etc) is not very useful when migrating files and/or
 > mirroring the layout.

Considering this in next version. Thanks

Patch Set 1:

> > "lfs getstripe" prints the stripe count/size/index of the last
 > > initialized component for these parameters on PFL files.  The
 > > presence of the earlier components (with fewer stripes, smaller
 > > stripe size, etc) is not very useful when migrating files and/or
 > > mirroring the layout.
 > 
 > Considering this in next version. Thanks

Note that if printing in YAML format, as opposed to just fetching a single value, it is possible to provide a more complex output like "lfs getstripe -y".  However, it probably makes sense to start with the single-valued -printf output, and then add the YAML output in a separate patch that handles more complex output formats, since it doesn't really look like the two output types share much of the code.

Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Abandoned

Abandoning this patch since new versions are available here - https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/45136/

commit.subject: LU-10378 utils: add formatted printf to lfs find
commit.message:
LU-10378 utils: add formatted printf to lfs find

Introduced two new options with lfs find utility:
1. '--yaml' (-y) that allows users to request lfs find to gather
all metadata related to the specified file/directory path.
2. '--printf' (-k) along with the format specifiers given below
that allow users to easily obtain metadata in formatted style.

How to use these options:
-------------------------
1. -y or --yaml: Required to print all the metadata information
in yaml format
2. -k or --printf : Specify this flag along with the format
specifiers to print selected information from the metadata.
Note: You still have to specify –y for this to work. Specify the
format specifiers without white spaces in between as shown in
the example command below.

"lfs find -y -mtime -1 -k %a%t%c%bt%b%s%U%G%p%P
/lustre/lustre/test_dir/

-----------------------------
Parameter  | Format Specifier
-----------------------------
atime      |   %a
mtime      |   %t
ctime      |   %c
btime      |   %bt
blocks     |   %b
size       |   %s
name       |   %p
uid        |   %U
gid        |   %G
project id |   %P
-----------------------------

Additionally users need to set any of these flags to
obtain all the metadata even if they set –y flag.
M: mtime, O: Obd or Ost, m: mdt-index, i: stripe-index, U: user,
G: group, B: btime, b: blocks, LFS_NEWERXY_OPT, A: atime,
c: stripe-count, s: size, LFS_POOL_OPT, S: stripe-size,
L: layout, LFS_COMP_COUNT_OPT, E: component-end, comp-end,
LFS_COMP_START_OPT, LFS_COMP_FLAGS_OPT, N: mirror-count,
LFS_LAYOUT_FOREIGN_OPT, LFS_MIRROR_STATE_OPT, z: extension-size,
LFS_PROJID_OPT, T: mdt-count, H: mdt-hash

Change-Id: Icb52dbdb8f5ef7703b6365b3182f5a757f55c8f0
Signed-off-by: Anjus George <georgea@ornl.gov>
Signed-off-by: Rick Mohr <mohrrf@ornl.gov>
